conflicts.label: Iran 2026 (Day 64)
conflicts.note: Operation Epic Fury — Day 64. Ceasefire Day 24; 60-day War Powers deadline (May 1) passed without Congressional authorization — Trump declared 'hostilities terminated' in letter to Hill, claim contested by Senate Democrats and Sen. Susan Collins (R-ME). Three U.S. CSGs in CENTCOM AOR (Lincoln Northern Arabian Sea, Ford Red Sea conducting routine flight ops, Bush transiting Horn of Africa) — first time since OIF 2003. Hormuz dual blockade continuing; 45 vessels redirected cumulative (CENTCOM May 1, +3 vs Apr 29). Brent $108.10 (52% above Feb 27 baseline of $71); WTI $101.05; AAA gas $4.392 (47% above $2.99 baseline). Apr 30 Senate WPR vote failed 50-47 (6th attempt) — Sen. Susan Collins crossed over for first time. Sen. Lisa Murkowski (R-AK) threatens AUMF when Senate returns from recess week of May 11. IRGC Aerospace Cdr Maj. Gen. Majid Mousavi (May 2): war resumption 'likely' (rhetorical, no kinetic action). Israel struck 9 southern Lebanese villages May 2; Lebanese MoPH cumulative 2,618 killed / 8,094 wounded since Mar 2. Iran Foundation of Martyrs Apr 19 cumulative 3,468 (official figure); Iran MoH 3,375; HRANA 3,636. ~3.2M Iran IDPs + 1M+ Lebanese displaced. U.S. KIA 15 (all-cause; Pentagon-official 13 combat-only); WIA 415 (CENTCOM published). Cumulative U.S. cost midpoint $53.5B; range $25B-$1T (Pentagon $25B floor → Watson/Harvard ~$1T ceiling including economic spillover). Day 64 represents the longest U.S. combat operation under sole executive authority since 1973 War Powers Resolution to collide with the 60-day statutory cutoff during the conflict and have the executive branch publicly declare hostilities 'terminated' to obviate the trigger.

conflicts.label: Iraq 2003 (Day 21)
conflicts.note: Operation Iraqi Freedom — "shock and awe" + ground invasion. 300,000 troops deployed.

conflicts.label: Libya 2011 (Day 21)
conflicts.note: Operation Odyssey Dawn / Unified Protector — NATO no-fly zone. Limited US role.

conflicts.label: Gulf 1991 (Day 21)
conflicts.note: Operation Desert Storm — 38-day air campaign + 100-hour ground war. 700,000 coalition troops.

sources: CRS Reports R44116 (Iraq), RL33110 (Gulf War), R41725 (Libya). Costs inflation-adjusted to 2026 dollars using BLS CPI-U.
